title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened after a burst water main flooded streets, disrupting traffic for two days.
Repair crews worked nonstop for 48 hours to replace a ruptured 12-inch pipe, originally thought to be 36 inches.
The burst pipe caused evacuations of nearby businesses, including three daycares, due to extensive flooding in the area.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, was indicted on three rape and 17 indecent assault charges involving 11 patients aged 20 to 60.
Todd allegedly committed the assaults between 2021 and 2023 at Charles River Medical Associates in Framingham.
Over 200 former patients have joined a class-action lawsuit against Todd following the allegations and initial charges in 2025.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul advises using a deep cycle or LiFePO battery and a voltage inverter for van conversion without a generator.
He recommends a rooftop solar panel with a voltage regulator for maintaining and charging the auxiliary battery.
Use a pure sine wave converter for electronics and appliances, ensuring power needs are evaluated at 1,000-2,000 watts.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against former Brockton choral director Matthew Cunningham were dropped after the victim declined to testify.
Cunningham was arrested in August 2025 for alleged trafficking and sexual conduct with an underage boy.
He was placed on paid leave from Brockton High School and is no longer employed there.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old boy were arrested in Charlestown for allegedly robbing an elderly woman at gunpoint.
Jordan is charged with armed masked robbery, receiving stolen property, and trespassing. The 14-year-old faces similar charges.
Police arrested the suspects after a short pursuit in Charlestown, recovering the victim's credit card during the arrest.

title: Would a Massachusetts income tax cut actually help you? Here’s what to know.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts may vote on reducing the state income tax rate from 5% to 4% this November.
The tax cut could lower revenue by $5.1 billion annually, affecting education, transportation, and health funding.
Higher-income households would benefit significantly more from the tax cut compared to lower-income families.

title: 3 ways Cade Cunningham’s absence could impact the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cade Cunningham is sidelined with a collapsed lung, missing crucial games as Celtics aim for the No. 1 seed.
Jaylen Brown's MVP chances may improve with Cunningham potentially missing eligibility due to insufficient games played.
Cunningham's absence could weaken the Pistons, affecting their playoff momentum as the Celtics gain strength.

title: Readers say this is Massachusetts’ most stressful commute — and it’s not I-93 North
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston.com readers deemed the Southeast Expressway (Rt. 93 Southbound) the most stressful commute in Massachusetts, surpassing I-93 North.
Nearly half of over 150 respondents highlighted the Southeast Expressway's congestion and outdated infrastructure as major concerns.
I-95/Route 128 in Waltham received 18% of votes, while the Zakim Bridge/I-93 North garnered only 13% in reader responses.

title: A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Medway Police seek the head of 'Kevin Skellington,' a 12-foot skeleton, stolen from behind Medway Recreation Center.
Kevin Skellington's head was last seen on Friday and the incident is being investigated as larceny.
Security camera footage is being reviewed; the public is urged to report any sightings or information.

title: Dropkick Murphys to play at ‘No Kings’ rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dropkick Murphys will perform at the 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common on March 28 from 2 to 4 p.m.
The rally, part of a national 'No Kings Day' of action, is organized by ACLU Massachusetts and Indivisible Mass Coalition.
The event coincides with Dropkick Murphys' tour promoting their album 'For The People,' themed around fighting injustice.
